steps.title: Identify the sensory action
steps.content: The decisive clue is “The keenest vision can't define” — the word describes something that eyesight attempts but fails to do. The surrounding context emphasizes perfect blending, meaning the boundary between the leaf and its reflection has become imperceptible.

steps.title: Match to visual perception
steps.content: The answer is perceive — the sharpest sight cannot perceive or make out the border line because the blending is so perfect. The other three choices describe unrelated actions or don't align with what vision does. That matches choice A — A is correct.
